summary: Completed comprehensive testing of P0 auth flow features: signup with birth_date field, login flow, location permission modal (Always/Ask/Never options), and floating nav bar. All 14 backend tests passed. Frontend signup and login flows work correctly with LocationPermissionModal appearing after new signup.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal backdrop intercepts pointer events during Playwright automation, requiring force=True clicks. Works fine for real users.

passed_tests: Backend: /api/auth/register - accepts birth_date field and returns it in response
Backend: /api/auth/register - returns location_permission=null for new users
Backend: /api/auth/login - returns token and user with location_permission field
Frontend: Signup form has full Birth Date field (type=date)
Frontend: Signup form fields: username, email, password, birthdate all present
Frontend: LocationPermissionModal appears after successful signup
Frontend: LocationPermissionModal has all 3 options: Always, Ask Every Time, Never
Frontend: Clicking location option closes modal and lands on home page
Frontend: Login flow works correctly with proper error messages
Frontend: Floating nav bar renders with Home, Vibe, Random, History, Comfort buttons
Frontend: Random picks modal opens and shows 3 movie recommendations
Frontend: User menu shows username after login
Frontend: Welcome toast appears after successful signup/login
critical_code_review_comments: Modal backdrop in AuthModal.js intercepts clicks - consider adding pointer-events-none to backdrop div or adjusting z-index
localStorage key 'flick_token' still used (vs 'chef_token') - minor inconsistency with app rename to 'Chef'
seed_data_creation: Created multiple test users with TEST_ prefix for backend testing, auto-generated unique user for frontend testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth flow P0 features complete and tested. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_auth.py covers all auth endpoints. Use force=True when clicking elements inside modals due to backdrop overlay. Test users are created with TEST_ prefix and can be cleaned up. Location permission is stored both in localStorage ('chef_location_perm') and backend. Existing test user: create fresh via signup since test_chef@example.com may not exist.
